items.title: California Environmental Law & Policy Update 5.22.26
items.abstract: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) on Monday proposed two rules rolling back certain regulations limiting per- and poly-fluoroalkyl substance (PFAS) levels under the Safe Drinking Water Act....By: Allen Matkins

items.title: EPA Proposes Rules to Extend Time to Comply with PFOA and PFOS Drinking Water Standards and Rescind Regulatory Determinations and Regulations for Other PFAS
items.abstract: On May 18, 2026, the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) announced two proposed rules. The first proposed rule would uphold the federal drinking water Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCL) for perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) and perfluorooctane sulfonic acid (PFOS) while providin

items.title: Federal Court Sets June 30 Deadline for FDA Response to PFAS-in-Food Citizen Petition
items.abstract: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) now faces a June 30, 2026, deadline to issue a final response to a citizen petition seeking enforceable per- and polyfluorinated alkyl substances (PFAS) limits in or on certain foods. The U.S. District Court for the District of Arizona issue

items.title: IRS Will Hold June Hearing on Petition to Add Methyl Methacrylate-Ethyl Methacrylate-Methacrylic Acid Copolymer in a Styrene Solution to List of Chemical Substances Subject to Superfund Excise Tax
items.abstract: The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) announced on May 18, 2026, that it will hold a hearing on June 18, 2026, on the notice of filing to add methyl methacrylate-ethyl methacrylate-methacrylic acid copolymer in a styrene solution to the list of taxable substances. 91 Fed. Reg. 28759

items.title: Michigan AG Secures Monsanto PCB Settlement
items.abstract: Michigan AG Dana Nessel secured a settlement with Monsanto Company, Solutia Inc., and Pharmacia LLC to resolve allegations that the companies’ PCB-containing products caused environmental contamination in Michigan....By: Cozen O'Connor

items.title: Microplastics In Drinking Water: What EPA’s Draft CCL 6 Means For The Consumer Products Industry
items.abstract: Overview -  EPA’s April 2026 draft Sixth Contaminant Candidate List (CCL 6) places microplastics on the federal regulatory radar for the first time, a milestone that consumer products companies cannot afford to ignore....By: Integral Consulting Inc.

items.title: PFAS Regulation and Litigation Continue to Evolve as Federal Deadlines Slip and States Press Ahead

items.title: EPA Releases Final Risk Evaluation for 1,2-Dichloroethane, Finds Unreasonable Risk of Injury to Human Health for 15 COUs Involving Workplace Exposure

items.title: Microplastics Move into the Federal Policy Pipeline: What Plastics and Chemicals Companies Need to Know Now

items.title: From Liability to Legacy: Leveraging CERCLA to Protect Public Water Systems
items.abstract: In April 2024, the U.S. EPA finalized National Primary Drinking Water Regulations, setting enforceable Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) for six PFAS, including a stringent 4.0 parts per trillion (ppt) for PFOA and PFOS....By: Napoli Shkolnik
